purpose: Idempotent entry-point that orchestrates the full WSL2 dev-environment installation by sourcing and running each module group in order, then wiring git hooks, registering a weekly cron job, and stamping the environment version.
invariants: REPO_ROOT is set to the directory containing bootstrap.sh and exported before any module is sourced
Running bootstrap.sh multiple times is safe — all operations are idempotent
DRY_RUN=1 (via --dry-run) causes every mutating operation to log its intent without executing
git hooksPath is set to $REPO_ROOT/hooks so pre-commit runs on every subsequent commit
A version stamp is always written to ~/tools/env-version on successful completion
gotchas: --only accepts comma-separated group names and replaces the entire default group list, so omitting a group skips its manifest entries
Module functions are resolved by convention (group name with hyphens replaced by underscores + _install suffix) — a missing function only warns, not errors
stdout and stderr are tee'd to a timestamped log file under ~/tools/logs/ for the full run
The weekly cron entry for devtools outdated is unconditionally rewritten on each bootstrap run, replacing any previous one

key_functions.name: main
key_functions.summary: Parses CLI flags, runs selected module groups, writes agent discovery files, registers cron, and stamps the version

key_functions.name: run_group
key_functions.summary: Sources a module file and calls its <group>_install function, warning if absent

key_functions.name: list_groups
key_functions.summary: Prints all known groups with their description strings for --list output
